title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following a dispute regarding a missing powder box, Colin McNaughton silences a group of arguing students by banging on a table, causing a jar of orange marmalade to shatter. Colin defends Sylvia, arguing that her actions were not stealing but the result of an emotional crisis, and that she requires sympathy. Genevieve insists the behavior was stealing, but Lenn Bateson interrupts the argument to leave for college. Other students, including Mr. Chandran, Rene, and Mr. Akibombo, also depart.

Mrs. Haberdash remains behind with her daughter, Valerie. Valerie reveals that she suspected Sylvia’s motives, suggesting Sylvia's actions were a way to get Colin's attention. Valerie also urges her mother to ensure Celia returns Genevieve's powder box to maintain peace. Celia enters the dining room in tears and attempts to compensate Mrs. Haberdash for the broken items and losses by writing a check. Because her pen is out of ink, Celia uses Nigel's green ink from a shelf to complete the transaction.

The Italian servant, Jerome, informs Mrs. Haberdash that the Countess Nicolletti is in a state of agitation. Mrs. Haberdash finds the Countess pacing her room and shouting. The Countess accuses Mrs. Haberdash of calling the police to her respectable dormitory. Mrs. Haberdash denies this, explaining that no one called the police; rather, a private detective who had been a guest for dinner the previous night had visited to give a lecture on criminology. The Countess counters by citing her past troubles with the police involving forged documents and a murder case.
